chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَقْطَعُ الصَّلَاةَ مِنَ الضَّحِكِ وَ الْحَدَثِ وَ الْإِشَارَةِ وَ النِّسْيَانِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 45 – What cuts off the Salāt, from the laugh, and the breaking of the ablution, and the gesture, and the forgetfulness, and other than that

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/239)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/239)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَأْخُذُهُ الرُّعَافُ وَ الْقَيْ‏ءُ فِي الصَّلَاةِ كَيْفَ يَصْنَعُ قَالَ يَنْفَتِلُ فَيَغْسِلُ أَنْفَهُ وَ يَعُودُ فِي صَلَاتِهِ فَإِنْ تَكَلَّمَ فَلْيُعِدْ صَلَاتَهُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ وُضُوءٌ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Abdullah Bin Aamir, from Ali Bin Mahziyar, from Fazalat, from Al A’ala, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the man seized by the nose-bleed and the vomiting during the Salāt, how he should react. Heasws said: ‘He should turn and wash his nose, then return to his Salāt. But if he speaks, so let him repeat his Salāt, and there would be no (repeat) ablution upon him’.
en.sarwar: 9. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn ‘Amir from Ali ibn Mahziyar from Fadalah from al-‘Ala’ from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who experiences nose bleeding and vomiting while in Salah (prayer). What is he to do? He (the Imam) said, ‘He turns to wash his nose and comes back to his Salah (prayer). If he has spoken, he must perform his Salah (prayer) again, but he does not need Wudu’.’”
